executive_summary: The account @geopolitics_truthseeker is assessed with moderate confidence as a probable proxy account amplifying Russian state narratives. The account primarily shares content from RT, Sputnik, and lesser-known proxy sites, with posting patterns suggesting automated or semi-automated operation.

key_findings.finding: Account consistently amplifies content from known Russian state media outlets (RT, Sputnik) and documented proxy sites, with 85% of shared links pointing to these sources.

evidence.reference: Link analysis: 170/200 shared URLs (85%) point to RT, Sputnik, or GEC-documented proxy sites

key_findings.finding: Posting patterns suggest automated or semi-automated operation with consistent 15-minute intervals between posts during active hours.

evidence.reference: Timing analysis shows posts at regular 15-minute intervals from 08:00-20:00 UTC

key_findings.finding: Account was created shortly before a major geopolitical event and immediately began posting high-volume content on that topic.

evidence.reference: Account created 2025-12-01, first post within 24 hours of creation

primary_narratives.narrative: Western sanctions are destroying European economies while Russia thrives
NATO expansion is the root cause of global instability

behavioral_indicators.description: Average 8 posts per day at regular 15-minute intervals
98% retweets and shared links, only 2% original content

network_context.amplification_patterns: Primarily amplifies RT and Sputnik content

source_attribution.content_origin: RT and Sputnik wire content, laundered through intermediary blogs

cannot_determine: Whether the account is operated by a human or fully automated
Organizational affiliation of the account operator
data_gaps: Account history limited to ~2 months since creation
No access to DM or private interaction data
alternative_interpretations: Account could be a genuine enthusiast who happens to share state media content
Regular posting intervals could be from a scheduling tool used by authentic users

evidence_references.reference: Link analysis: 85% of shared URLs point to state media or proxy sites
evidence_references.notes: Matched against GEC proxy site list
